observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct a thorough reconciliation of the documents maintained by their respective offices to determine the correct classification of each PPE account and subsequently, effect the reclassification and adjustments, where necessary, to ensure the validity and accuracy of the records on or before June 30, 2025, and every December 31 thereafter.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee, consistently carry out the complete annual physical inventory of PPE and, promptly prepare and submit the result of its count to both the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant on or before January 31 of each year.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office submit the complete list of settlement of the disallowances together with the supporting documents and a reconciliation thereof no later than April 30, 2025 to serve as a basis for the Audit Team in the issuance of the Notice of Settlement of Suspension/Disallowance/Charge (NSSDC).

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal General Services Officer request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries, duly supported by Property Transfer Receipt for those to be transferred to other levels of government, and Deed of Donation for private organizations; and
Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Vouchers for removing the affected PPE accounts from the books of accounts upon receipt of the SB authorization, Property Transfer Receipt, and Deed of Donation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant demand from the accountable officers with outstanding cash advances, as of December 31, 2024, the immediate liquidation of their cash advances, citing Section 5 of CSC MC No. 23, s. 2019, and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
Furthermore, we reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant refrain from allowing the grant of additional cash advances to accountable officers with outstanding balances, especially to non-permanently appointed officials, who are only allowed under the rules to draw cash advances for their travel expenses. Otherwise, all approving officials shall be held jointly and severally liable for the unsettled amounts.
We also reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant conduct re-orientation to all officials and employees on the policies and procedures on the granting and liquidation of cash advances, underscoring the sanctions to be imposed on erring officials and employees as provided under CSC MC No. 23, s.2019 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” on the face of the Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents to prevent the possibility of reuse; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that the face of the Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents are stamped “PAID” to prevent the possibility of reuse.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and Local Finance Committee exercise due diligence in approving and monitoring the appropriations and commitments of the LGU.
We also reiterated and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer closely monitor the cash balance against expenditures to be incurred to avoid cash overdraft; that when it becomes apparent during the year that there will be an under-collection of estimated revenues, limit the disbursement of funds for the remaining months only to the extent of realized income covered with actual cash.
Finally, we reiterated and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer review the cash position of the Trust Fund, establish the exact amount needed to be restituted to ensure availability of funds to be used for its intended purposes.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Local Finance Committee ensure that sufficient details of the project and cost are provided in the succeeding appropriations ordinance, most especially projects funded under 20% Development Fund.
We also reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor secure from the Sangguniang Bayan a resolution accepting, confirming and ratifying all contracts entered into by the Local Chief Executive for the implementation of projects sourced from lumpsum appropriations in CY 2024, with a copy furnished to the Audit Team on or before April 30, 2025.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal DRRM Council, as well as the Municipal Accountant, properly allocate and utilize the 30% Quick Response Fund of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in accordance with RA 10121 and all applicable rules and regulations to ensure the realization of the purpose for which the fund is intended. Otherwise, disbursements made not in accordance with the applicable law and regulations shall be disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that a copy of the report of inspection or its equivalent shall be submitted to the Head of the Auditing Unit within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered and, in the case of unserviceable and disposable property/assets, immediately after inspection thereof by management.
observations.reason: The copies of inspection and acceptance reports were still not submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for CY 2015 and for the years thereafter and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office take the following courses of action:

No cash advance should be granted to accountable officers with existing cash advances; and

Cash advances should be granted only at amounts covered by the fidelity bonds of the accountable officers.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR (Finding No. 9)

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended that the BAC, through its Secretariat, post or cause to be posted the APP, the PMR and the List of Observers on the On-Line Monitoring Evaluation System (OMES) of the GPPB. 

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipality strive to create and maintain its own website so as to fulfill the transparency requirement of the procurement law.
observations.reason:
The APP, PMR and the List of Observers were still not posted in the GPPB OMES.

The Municipality has no own website.

observations.recommendation: Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant enjoin the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees to settle their long unliquidated cash advances with and without Notice of Withholding Orders.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the department heads’ concerned to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

Recommendations: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor to consider the deadline in the submission of the RPCPPPE in scheduling the conduct of the annual physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the local government as of December 31 of each year to ensure the submission of the report on time.
Likewise, upon finishing the on-going physical count, we recommended that after the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office have reconciled the balances of the property, plant and equipment per RPCPPE and the year-end financial statements, to immediately submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly prepared and certified correct by the inventory committee to the office of the audit team leader for the attestation and for evaluation.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) pertaining to all NGA-funded projects of the Municipality and should exert all reasonable efforts and diligence in securing said contracts/ Memorandum of Agreements in a timely manner from the source agency.

Recommendation: We recommended that the officials and employees concerned adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1 on appropriating and utilizing the 20% DF for development projects.

Recommendations:  We recommended that:
The MACCO and the MEO reconcile their records to determine the status and correct balances of the affected CIP accounts;
The MEO provide the MACCO with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents to serve as the basis for the transfer of these projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
Based on the adjusted data, the MACCO computes the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.

Recommendation: We recommended that the officials and employees concerned adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1 on appropriating and utilizing the 20% DF for development project in order to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to immediately submit the Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for the period covered December 31, 2017 to present except May 2018 using the format provided by COA Circular No. 2012-02 under Annex B and to timely submit the said report from now on.

Recommendation: We recommended that management intensify its efforts to update its ESWMP for submission and approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of preserving our natural resources by proper disposal of wastes.

Recommendation: We recommended that an annual transfer of excess collections over payments made for the honoraria and overtime pay of the members of the BAC should be made to be recorded as income in the General Fund. Likewise, stop utilizing such collections for purposes other than payment of honoraria and overtime pay.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appropriate funds and hire competent personnel to man the Economic Enterprise Office. As the Local Chief Executive, ensure that all departments exercise their duties and responsibilities in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.
Recommendation: We recommended that the MHRMO and MACCO ensure that all payrolls be duly supported with complete documentary requirements as enumerated under COA Circular 2012-001.

Recommendation:  We recommended that the MACCO submits the list of settlement of the disallowances together with the supporting documents as well as a reconciliation thereof to serve as a basis of the audit team in the issuance of the NSSDC.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO and MACCO to regularly and accurately deduct from each official/employee the GSIS premium and loan amortizations and to prohibit such official/employee from having the liberty of deciding when and how much deductions shall be made.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to abide by the requirement of Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-01 dated March 18, 2014 for timely evaluation of the LGU’s GAD Accomplishment Reports.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the MEO particularly the project engineers to religiously install the billboards following the required specifications of tarpaulins/billboards for infrastructure projects as stated under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
We also recommended that Management exert efforts to create its own website to give the public access to information and promote full transparency of PPAs undertaken by the municipality.

Recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMC revisit the above mentioned laws, rules and regulations and refrain from including in the LDRRMF charges which does not conformed to such law, rules and regulations. Instead, assess the adequacy and availability of basic search, rescue, response and other equipment useful for disaster prevention, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery of the municipality.

Recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials to prioritize the Solid Waste Management project/program/activities in line with its ten-year period plan through its inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and appropriation of funds for its implementation.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office and other implementing departments to prepare comprehensive planning of all project proposals before submission to the NGAs for funding requests and for efficient project implementation.
Likewise, we recommended that fund for project duplication project and not feasible for implementation; inform the source agency, request for the realignment and fast track the implementation of all NGA-funded projects to avoid return of funds to source agency.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office to closely monitor and ensure economic and efficient implementation of all SALINTUBIG projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement voucher together with the supporting documents of payment made to Ecomixed Construction and Development Corporation. And the MEO submit the required documents to our office (approved extension letter, extra work order, etc.), as the case maybe, relative to the noted delays/suspensions/extensions of the above-cited projects.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit the updated monitoring report to the audit team and to provide adequate support to the remaining recipients to enable them to reap the projects benefits.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Municipal Mayor provide appropriation to ensure that the above projects will be completed to prevent further deterioration of the existing structure.
We also recommended that the management only identify projects which they could provide sufficient appropriation for the construction and completion.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accounting Office:

To submit immediately to our Office the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) together with the Bank Statements, credit/debit memos and paid checks of the deposit of all the depository accounts maintained by the Municipality for the periods/months of April to December 2017.

To religiously comply with the prescribed period of the submission of the BRS and its supporting documents as prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 which is on fifteenth days following the end of the month. 

To determine the reason behind the late/non-submission and provide proper and immediately measures to remedy the problem.
observations.reason: Although the MACCO already submitted the BRS for CY 2018, it was noted that the submission was not regularly made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor, being the primarily responsible for all government funds and property of the Municipality, to direct the Municipal Accounting Office: 

To submit immediately to our Office the trial balances, quarterly financial statements, report of collections and deposits, report of checks issued, disbursement vouchers, and liquidation reports for the periods of August to December 2017.

To religiously comply with the prescribed period of the submission of the required reports and its supporting documents as prescribed in the COA rules and regulations.

To determine the reason behind the late/non-submission and provide proper and immediately measures to remedy the existing problem.
observations.reason: Submission was not regularly made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require:

The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office and other concerned departments, to update the balances in the ledger to reflect whether there has been unrecorded disbursements pertaining to each individual projects. 

After the update, the Municipal Treasurer either to return the unutilized balance to the source agency or deposit the amount to the National Treasury.
observations.reason: There are still exists unexpended balances not reverted to the National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to initiate, with the coordination of the Sangguniang Bayan, the creation of permanent positions for the mandatory positions of the MDRRMO and to ensure strict compliance to the position titles, qualification standards, salary grades and budgetary requirements provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Instruct the MDRRMO to obtain a list of all the volunteers from government and non-government agencies/institutions/organizations engaged by the Municipality and submit the list to the OCD for accreditation and inclusion in the database of ACDVs. 

Enjoin the MDRRMO to coordinate with the OCD regarding the accreditation process for the disaster volunteers, to undertake the accreditation process and to issue the corresponding identification cards in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and/or process consistent with the provisions of the R.A. No. 10121.
observations.reason: According to an MDRRMO staff, the OCD do not have the guidelines in the accreditation of volunteers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO), the Municipal Agriculturist Office, and other concerned departments:

To determine the reason for the non-implementation of the projects and programs under 20% Development Fund for CY 2017 and provide measures to remedy the problems to pave the way for the immediate implementation thereof. 

To ensure economic and efficient implementation of projects and programs funded by the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Some projects remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct:

The Municipal Accounting Office to record all assets procured, pertaining to projects funded by national government agencies and to be implemented by beneficiary civil society organizations (CSOs), under their appropriate asset accounts in the books of the Municipality; and

The PESO Manager-Designate to submit the pertinent memorandum of agreements entered into between the Municipality and beneficiary CSOs supporting the projects funded by national government agencies and to be implemented with the CSOs.
observations.reason: Assets were transferred without cost to CSOs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible officials of the Municipality to provide the Audit Team with a copy of the ordinance approving the 5-Year Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (MDRRMF) to justify the lump-sum appropriation of the Fund in the 2016 Annual Budget. 

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible officials of the Municipality to specifically provide in its annual budget, in detail, the programs/projects/activities and objects of expenditures into which the 5% Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (MDRRMF) shall be applied and avoid lump-sum appropriations except for the 30% Quick Response Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible officials of the Municipality to accurately allocate 30% of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (MDRRMF) as Quick Response Fund (QRF) to effectively answer for the relief and recovery projects and activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accounting Office to transfer the unexpended balance of the Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality within the next five (5) years.

We recommended that the responsible officials and employees of the Municipality observe prudence in the discharge of their duties so as government funds are appropriately safeguarded such as thru the following measures:

In case a Letter of Credit shall be issued by the Municipality, the BAC shall see to it that the terms in the opening of the same conforms with the requirements of Section 42.5 of the Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184 and if the winning bidder is a local supplier, the same should be issued to the latter and not to its foreign manufacturer or supplier.

No payment shall be made on a Letter of Credit unless the goods have been delivered and accepted as certified by the Municipality in accordance with the delivery schedule provided for in the contract.

The Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office provide explanation on the non-imposition of liquidated damages on the delays committed by the supplier on the delivery of the 20 units brand new mini dump trucks and collect the same from the supplier.


The General Services Office to submit the lacking documents as follows:
For imported items:
Consular Invoice/Pro-forma invoice of the foreign supplier with the corresponding details
Home Consumption Value of the items
Breakdown of the expenses incurred in the Importation
Original copy of Dealer’s/Supplier’s Invoices showing the quantity, description of the articles, unit and total value, duly signed by the dealer or his representative, and indicating receipt by the proper agency official of items delivered.
Tax receipts from the Bureau of Customs or the BIR indicating the exact specifications and/or serial number of the equipment procured by the government as proof of payment of all taxes and duties due on the same equipment, supplied or sold to the government [Administrative Order (AO) No. 200 dated November 21, 1990]

Henceforth, payments made to suppliers should be supported with the necessary documentary requirements particularly the sales invoice.

The Municipal Accounting Office to require the supplier, CONEQUIP PHILIPPINES, INC. to provide the Municipality the necessary warranty security on the equipment procured, either in the form of cash equivalent to at least 10% of the P30,000,000.00 paid or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least ten percent (10%) of the total contract price of P29,999,682.00.

The Municipal Accounting Office to require the supplier, CONEQUIP PHILIPPINES, INC. to provide the Municipality the necessary withholding taxes amounting to P1,607,125.82 on the equipment procured not deducted on the contract price of P29,999,682.00.

The Municipal Accounting Office to adjust the erroneous entry made on the non-existing “Other Receivable” of P1,748,978.84 and P318.00 overstatement in the “Construction and Heavy Equipment” account and submit accordingly the adjusting journal entry to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: No subsequent transactions requiring letter of credit. The LGU committed to implement such measure for future transactions.

Likewise, we recommended that collection of revenues be assigned only to regular employees of the Municipality or if warranted by funds, the Municipality could consider the regularization of the Job Order personnel assigned to perform duties of permanent employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Mayor should instruct all heads of offices in-charge in development projects to monitor implementation of proposed programs, projects and activities which are funded by the 20% Development Fund at all levels. Likewise, it is imperative to ensure that funds allocated thereon are utilized for projects identified in the approved AIP, according to the guidelines/policies prescribed under the prevailing DILG/DBM Joint Circular No.2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Any unexpended appropriation balance arising from the completed projects can be re-programmed to other programs, project, and activities as approved by the Sanggunian. Likewise, submit a proposed revision/changes effected in the AIP for CY 2016, supplemental appropriation to support the appropriation for additional projects included in the SAAOB, if any, citing the details of the PPAs such as project titles, costs and its corresponding appropriation, obligation and the unexpended appropriation balance.

Explain in writing the specific causes/contributory factors to the delay of unimplemented projects.

The concerned offices in-charge of the implementation of the proposed and approved PPAs should submit complete information on status of projects embodied in the AIP for CY 2016 and in succeeding quarterly reports as to the extent of implementation of PPAs.

The report should include vital information in assessing whether the PPAs are efficiently and effectively implemented such as project title, estimated costs, appropriation, obligation incurred, appropriation balance, starting date and target date of completion, percentage of completion, actual cost incurred to date as well as other information indicating the status of the PPA implementation. In addition, submit the statement of work accomplished for projects as well as the program of works for projects which are either funded by the Current and Continuing Appropriation/Allotment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the  Municipal  Mayor  to  direct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office:

   a.   To  submit  immediately  to  our  Office  the  Bank   Reconciliation
      Statements (BRS) together with the Bank Statements, credit/debit memos
      and paid  checks  of  the  deposit  of  all  the  depository  accounts
      maintained by the Municipality for the  periods/months   of  April  to
      December 2017.


   b.  To religiously comply with the prescribed period of the submission of
      the BRS and its supporting documents as prescribed under COA  Circular
      No. 96-011 which is on fifteenth days following the end of the month.
   c. To determine the reason behind  the  late/non-submission  and  provide
      proper and immediately measures to remedy the problem.

We  recommended  the  Municipal  Mayor  to  consider  the  deadline  in  the
submission of the RPCPPPE in scheduling the conduct of the  annual  physical
inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the  local  government  as
of December 31 of each year to ensure the submission of the report on time.

Likewise, upon finishing the on-going  physical  count,  we  recommend  that
after the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting  Office  have
reconciled the balances of the property, plant and equipment per RPCPPE  and
the year-end financial statements, to immediately submit the Report  on  the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly  prepared  and
certified correct by the inventory committee to  the  office  of  the  audit
team leader for the attestation and for evaluation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, being the primarily responsible for  all
government funds and property of the Municipality, to direct  the  Municipal
Accounting Office:

   a. To submit immediately to our  Office  the  trial  balances,  quarterly
      financial statements, report of collections and  deposits,  report  of
      checks issued, disbursement vouchers, and liquidation reports for  the
      periods of August to December 2017.


   b. To religiously comply with the prescribed period of the submission  of
      the required reports and its supporting documents as prescribed in the
      COA rules and regulations.


   c. To determine the reason behind  the  late/non-submission  and  provide
      proper and immediately measures to remedy the existing problem.

To eliminate the above cycle of deliberate disregard of the laws, rules  and
regulations concerning the granting, utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
advances,  we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  the  Municipal
Accountant immediately take the following measures:

   a. Direct all accountable officers with  outstanding  cash  advances  for
      travel and special purposes to settle and liquidate immediately  their
      outstanding cash advances.


   b. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers
      with existing cash advances.


   c. Cause the withholding of salary or other  benefits  of  the  concerned
      accountable officers to satisfy long overdue obligations.


   d. The Municipal Accountant  issue  certification  of  no  previous  cash
      advance for every cash advance to be availed by  accountable  officers
      and attached the same to disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the management implement the following measures:

   a. The incumbent Municipal Mayor liquidate immediately his  long  overdue
      special cash advances.


   b. The Municipal Accountant  see  to  it  that  only  cash  advances  for
      travelling expenses are granted to elective officials.


   c. The  Municipal  Accountant  follow  up,  thru  a  demand  letter,  the
      liquidation of the special cash  advance  of  former  Municipal  Mayor
      Joseph Nilo F. Parreñas.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

   a. Direct Municipal Treasurer, Hermes E. Cosmod, and  Special  Disbursing
      Officer,  Flordelina  R.  Marial,  to  liquidate   immediately   their
      outstanding cash advances;


   b. Designate other qualified special  disbursing  officers  who  will  be
      responsible  and  accountable  for  palay  and  vermicast-buying  cash
      advances “only”; and


   c. Instruct the duly designated special disbursing officers to  liquidate
      their cash advances immediately after the paying the farmers.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require:

   1)  The  Municipal  Accountant,  in  coordination  with   the   Municipal
      Engineering Office and other  concerned  departments,  to  update  the
      balances in the ledger to reflect whether there  has  been  unrecorded
      disbursements pertaining to each individual projects.


   2) After the  update,  the  Municipal  Treasurer  either  to  return  the
      unutilized balance to the source agency or deposit the amount  to  the
      National Treasury.

We  recommended  the  Local  School  Board   to   carefully   evaluate   the
programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF  Budget  taking  into
consideration the specific PPAs set forth under Section 1 of  R.A  No.  5447
and Sections 100 (c) 272 of R.A. No.  7160  and  to  stop  the  charging  of
expenditures pertaining  to  the  Alternative  Leaning  System  against  the
Special Education Fund unless be allowed  by  law.  Otherwise,  expenditures
not falling within those allowed under the said laws shall be disallowed  in
audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to take the following courses of action:

   1. No cash  advance  should  be  granted  to  accountable  officers  with
      existing cash advances.


   2. Cash advances should  be  granted  only  at  amounts  covered  by  the
      fidelity bonds of the accountable officers.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor  to  instruct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to:

   a) Maintain a separate bank account for SWM Fund in accordance  with  the
      provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management (ESWM) Board under
      Chapter 11.3 Funding Options of  the  Municipality’s  10-Year  Revised
      Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.


   b) To record all transactions pertaining to Solid Waste Management in the
      special account for the Solid Waste Management Fund  particularly  the
      receipts of funds from all sources and its utilization.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor  to  instruct  the  Local  Disaster  Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to immediately submit  the  Monthly  Report
on Utilization  of  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and  Management  Fund  as  of
December 31, 2017 using the format provided  by  COA  Circular  No.  2012-02
under Annex B and to timely submit the said report from now on.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to initiate,  with  the  coordination  of
the  Sangguniang  Bayan,  the  creation  of  permanent  positions  for   the
mandatory positions of the MDRRMO and to ensure  strict  compliance  to  the
position  titles,  qualification  standards,  salary  grades  and  budgetary
requirements provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC  Joint  Memorandum  Circular
No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

   a) Instruct the MDRRMO to obtain  a  list  of  all  the  volunteers  from
      government  and   non-government   agencies/institutions/organizations
      engaged by the Municipality  and  submit  the  list  to  the  OCD  for
      accreditation and inclusion in the database of ACDVs.


   b)  Enjoin  the  MDRRMO  to  coordinate  with  the  OCD   regarding   the
      accreditation process for the disaster volunteers,  to  undertake  the
      accreditation process and to issue  the  corresponding  identification
      cards in accordance with  the  prescribed  guidelines  and/or  process

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  the following courses of action in order to achieve program objectives and to strengthen the internal control in the management and operations of the venture:

Accounting transactions of the special project shall follow the prescribed provisions of the New Government Accounting Systems. Accurate Financial Reports such as the Balance Sheet, the Statement of Income and Expenses as well as the Statement of Cash Flows shall be prepared for the interested concerned authority to be informed of the condition and operation of the economic enterprise.
The local government shall provide authority through legislation the management of the project. The existing   practice provides compensation for the personnel handling the multifarious activities on honorarium basis ranging from P5,600.00 to P12,000.00. These personnel are handling not only property but material cash accountabilities of the economic enterprise of the municipality and these personnel are not properly bonded.  
While the project designs so provide some mechanics on the operation of the agri-business, this shall be sanctioned by the Legislative Body. There shall be some basis for the authority of the personnel of the ABE-FAP on the interest rates imposed on the lending activity, pricing of the goods being purchased as well as the selling price of goods being sold to the market.
There shall be a  budget for the operation of the special project which shall be formulated by a special body duly approved by the law making body of the municipality
Accountable forms used such as the Commodity Price Invoice, the Delivery Receipts, the Trust Receipt Agreement and all other forms shall be sanctioned   by proper authority.  
The accounting shall cause the adjustments of the records relative to this economic enterprise in proper coordination with the ABE-FAP personnel so that reconciliation of accounts are made and effected properly. Thereafter, periodic reconciliation of records between the two offices shall be conducted regularly.

Accurate Financial Reports shall be submitted accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommend that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) together with  the
Bank  Statements,  credit/debit  memos  and  paid  checks  of  the   deposit
maintained under DBP Current Account  No.  0916-006491-030  and  henceforth,
submit all monthly BRS and supporting documents of the  depository  accounts
of  the  Municipality  within  the  prescribed  period  provided  under  COA
Circular No. 96-011 in order to determine the correctness  of  the  cash  in
bank balance appearing in the financial statements.

Recommendations:

We recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office and the General Services Office to reconcile the amount of property,
plant and equipment  per  RPCPPE  and  financial  statements  in  order  to
eliminate the variance noted.

Likewise, we recommend that the General Services Officer group the items  in
the RPCPPE in accordance with  their  classifications  in  order  to  easily
determine their total amount.

Recommendations:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct  the  responsible
official(s) of the Municipality  to  submit  the  Memorandum  of  Agreements
(MOAs) pertaining to all BuB-funded projects of the Municipality for the  FY
2015 and 2016.

Henceforth, all  MOAs  covering  fund  transfers  from  national  government
agencies should be  submitted  to  the  Audit  Team  within  the  prescribed
period.

Recommendations:

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct:

   1. The  Municipal  Accounting  Office  to  record  all  assets  procured,
      pertaining to projects funded by national government agencies  and  to
      be implemented by  beneficiary  civil  society  organizations  (CSOs),
      under  their  appropriate  asset  accounts  in  the   books   of   the
      Municipality; and


   2. The PESO Manager-Designate  to  submit  the  pertinent  memorandum  of
      agreements entered into between the Municipality and beneficiary  CSOs
      supporting the projects funded by national government agencies and  to
      be implemented with the CSOs.

Recommendations:

We recommend that the Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to strictly observe compliance with the  perpetual  inventory  method
of accounting on  the  supplies  and  materials  procured  for  construction
projects  by  recording  all  purchases  under  the   account   Construction
Materials Inventory.

Likewise, effect the necessary adjustment for the  amount  of  supplies  and
materials which remained unutilized for the intended projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the  responsible  officials  of
the Municipality to strictly observe the pertinent  provisions  of  DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the  appropriation  and  utilization
of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that said fund is  optimally  utilized
and no irregular expenditures are charged against the fund.

Recommendations:

We recommend that:

   1.  The  Mayor  should  instruct  all  heads  of  offices  in-charge   in
      development projects to monitor implementation of  proposed  programs,
      projects and activities which are funded by the 20%  Development  Fund
      at all levels.  Likewise,  it  is  imperative  to  ensure  that  funds
      allocated thereon are utilized for projects identified in the approved
      AIP,  according  to  the  guidelines/policies  prescribed  under   the
      prevailing DILG/DBM Joint Circular No.2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.


   2. Any  unexpended  appropriation  balance  arising  from  the  completed
      projects  can  be  re-programmed  to  other  programs,  project,   and
      activities as approved by the Sanggunian. Likewise, submit a  proposed
      revision/changes  effected  in  the  AIP  for  CY  2016,  supplemental
      appropriation to support the  appropriation  for  additional  projects
      included in the SAAOB, if any, citing the details of the PPAs such  as
      project titles, costs and its corresponding appropriation,  obligation
      and the unexpended appropriation balance.


   3. Explain in writing the specific  causes/contributory  factors  to  the
      delay of unimplemented projects.


   4. The concerned offices in-charge of the implementation of the  proposed
      and approved PPAs should submit  complete  information  on  status  of
      projects embodied in the AIP for CY 2016 and in  succeeding  quarterly
      reports as to the extent of implementation of PPAs.


      The report should include vital information in assessing  whether  the
      PPAs are efficiently  and  effectively  implemented  such  as  project
      title,   estimated   costs,   appropriation,   obligation    incurred,
      appropriation balance, starting date and target  date  of  completion,
      percentage of completion, actual cost incurred  to  date  as  well  as
      other information indicating the status of the PPA implementation.  In
      addition, submit the statement of work accomplished  for  projects  as
      well as the program of works for projects which are either  funded  by
      the Current and Continuing Appropriation/Allotment.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible officials  of
the Municipality to provide the Audit Team with  a  copy  of  the  ordinance
approving the 5-Year Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and  Management  Fund
(MDRRMF) to justify the lump-sum appropriation  of  the  Fund  in  the  2016
Annual Budget.

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  responsible
officials of the Municipality to specifically provide in its annual  budget,
in detail, the  programs/projects/activities  and  objects  of  expenditures
into which the 5% Municipal Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and  Management  Fund
(MDRRMF) shall be applied and avoid lump-sum appropriations except  for  the
30% Quick Response Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible officials  of
the Municipality to accurately allocate 30% of the Municipal  Disaster  Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (MDRRMF)  as  Quick  Response  Fund  (QRF)  to
effectively answer for the relief and recovery projects  and  activities  of
the Municipality.

Recommendation:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct   the   Municipal
Accounting Office to transfer the unexpended balance of the  Quick  Response
Fund and DRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund  solely  for  the  purpose  of
supporting  disaster  risk  reduction  and  management  activities  of   the
Municipality within the next five (5) years.

Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of  the  Municipal  Solid
Waste  Management  Board,  to  direct  the  responsible  officials  of   the
Municipality    to    look    for    ways    to    adequately    fund    the
programs/projects/activities, such as but not limited to the provision of  a
sanitary landfill, which are necessary to fully implement the provisions  of
the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

Recommendations:

We recommend that the Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to establish the Municipal Solid Waste Management Fund as  a  special
account in the General Fund and accordingly, prepare the  required  separate
financial reports on the Fund.

We  recommend  that  the  Local  School   Board   carefully   evaluate   the
programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF  Budget  taking  into
consideration the specific PPAs set forth under Section 1 of  R.A  No.  5447
and Sections 100 (c) 272 of  R.A.  No.  7160.  Otherwise,  expenditures  not
falling within those allowed under the said  laws  shall  be  disallowed  in
audit.

Recommendations:

We  recommend  that  the  responsible  officials  and   employees   of   the
Municipality observe prudence  in  the  discharge  of  their  duties  so  as
government funds are appropriately safeguarded such as  thru  the  following
measures:

   1. In case a Letter of Credit shall be issued by  the  Municipality,  the
      BAC shall see to it that the terms in the opening of the same conforms
      with the requirements of Section 42.5 of the Revised IRR of  the  R.A.
      No. 9184 and if the winning bidder  is  a  local  supplier,  the  same
      should be issued to the latter and not to its foreign manufacturer  or
      supplier.


   2. No payment shall be made on a Letter of Credit unless the  goods  have
      been delivered and  accepted  as  certified  by  the  Municipality  in
      accordance with the delivery schedule provided for in the contract.


   3. The Municipal  Accounting  Office  and  the  General  Services  Office
      provide explanation on the non-imposition of liquidated damages on the
      delays committed by the supplier on the delivery of the 20 units brand
      new mini dump trucks and collect the same from the supplier.


   4. The General  Services  Office  to  submit  the  lacking  documents  as
      follows:
      • For imported items:
         o Consular Invoice/Pro-forma invoice of the foreign supplier  with
           the corresponding details
         o Home Consumption Value of the items
         o Breakdown of the expenses incurred in the Importation
      • Original copy of Dealer’s/Supplier’s Invoices showing the quantity,
        description of the articles, unit and total value, duly  signed  by
        the dealer or his representative, and  indicating  receipt  by  the
        proper agency official of items delivered.
      • Tax receipts from the Bureau of Customs or the BIR  indicating  the
        exact specifications and/or serial number of the equipment procured
        by the government as proof of payment of all taxes and  duties  due
        on  the  same  equipment,  supplied  or  sold  to  the   government
        [Administrative Order (AO) No. 200 dated November 21, 1990]


      Henceforth, payments made to suppliers should be  supported  with  the
      necessary documentary requirements particularly the sales invoice.


   5. The Municipal Accounting Office  to  require  the  supplier,  CONEQUIP
      PHILIPPINES, INC. to provide the Municipality the  necessary  warranty
      security on the  equipment  procured,  either  in  the  form  of  cash
      equivalent to at least 10% of the P30,000,000.00  paid  or  a  special
      bank guarantee equivalent to at least ten percent (10%) of  the  total
      contract price of P29,999,682.00.


   6. The Municipal Accounting Office  to  require  the  supplier,  CONEQUIP
      PHILIPPINES,  INC.  to  provide   the   Municipality   the   necessary
      withholding taxes amounting to P1,607,125.82 on the equipment procured
      not deducted on the contract price of P29,999,682.00.


   7. The Municipal Accounting Office to adjust the erroneous entry made  on
      the non-existing  “Other  Receivable”  of  P1,748,978.84  and  P318.00
      overstatement in the “Construction and Heavy  Equipment”  account  and
      submit accordingly the adjusting journal entry to the Audit Team.

To eliminate the above cycle of deliberate disregard of the laws, rules  and
regulations concerning the granting, utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
advances,  we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  the  Municipal
Accountant immediately take the following measures:

   1. Direct all accountable officers with  outstanding  cash  advances  for
      travel and special purposes to settle and liquidate immediately  their
      outstanding cash advances;


   2. No cash  advance  should  be  granted  to  accountable  officers  with
      existing cash advances; and


   3. Cause the withholding of salary or other  benefits  of  the  concerned
      accountable officers to satisfy long overdue obligations.

Recommendations:

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  instruct  the  Municipal
Accounting Office to submit immediately to the Audit  Team  the  Reports  of
Checks Issued with Disbursement Voucher  and  Liquidation  Reports  for  the
months of September to December 2016.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office should observe  the  submission,
within the prescribed period, of the Report  of  Collections  and  Deposits,
Disbursement Vouchers, Liquidation Reports together with the  Journal  Entry
Vouchers and general journal transactions to the Audit Team.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  direct   the   Municipal
Accounting Office to  submit  the  lacking  Notes  to  Financial  Statements
immediately and henceforth, submit the  year-end  financial  statements  and
supporting schedules of the Municipality on or before February  14  of  each
year to the Audit Team.

Likewise,  submit  the  monthly  trial  balance  and   quarterly   financial
statements on or before the prescribed period to the Audit Team.

We recommended that an explanation be provided to justify and/or elaborate the noted variance. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit within five days any Variation Order, together with the required supporting documents, to the Audit Team for the timely review of the same.

We recommended that the transfer of funds to other LGUs be accounted for in accordance with the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 94-013 wherein a Memorandum of Agreement is entered into by the parties involved and the amount transferred is accounted for as receivable subject to liquidation. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the amount of P555,000.00 transferred to the Provincial Government of Davao del Norte under the account “Due from Local Government Units (1-03-03-030)” and require the liquidation of the same from the latter.

We recommended that the Municipality transfer funds only to duly accredited Non-Governmental Organization/People’s Organization using the criteria set under COA Circular No. 2007-001 and a Memorandum of Agreement between the parties involved be executed in order to have terms of reference for the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for CY 2015 and for the years thereafter and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  the following courses of action in order to achieve program objectives and to strengthen the internal control in the management and operations of the venture:

Accounting transactions of the special project shall follow the prescribed provisions of the New Government Accounting Systems. Accurate Financial Reports such as the Balance Sheet, the Statement of Income and Expenses as well as the Statement of Cash Flows shall be prepared for the interested concerned authority to be informed of the condition and operation of the economic enterprise.
The local government shall provide authority through legislation the management of the project. The existing   practice provides compensation for the personnel handling the multifarious activities on honorarium basis ranging from P5,600.00 to P12,000.00. These personnel are handling not only property but material cash accountabilities of the economic enterprise of the municipality and these personnel are not properly bonded.  
While the project designs so provide some mechanics on the operation of the agri-business, this shall be sanctioned by the Legislative Body. There shall be some basis for the authority of the personnel of the ABE-FAP on the interest rates imposed on the lending activity, pricing of the goods being purchased as well as the selling price of goods being sold to the market.
There shall be a  budget for the operation of the special project which shall be formulated by a special body duly approved by the law making body of the municipality
Accountable forms used such as the Commodity Price Invoice, the Delivery Receipts, the Trust Receipt Agreement and all other forms shall be sanctioned   by proper authority.  
The accounting shall cause the adjustments of the records relative to this economic enterprise in proper coordination with the ABE-FAP personnel so that reconciliation of accounts are made and effected properly. Thereafter, periodic reconciliation of records between the two offices shall be conducted regularly.

Accurate Financial Reports shall be submitted accordingly.
